outlets.name: Washington Post
outlets.headline: Trump moves to declare Iran war over as 60-day War Powers deadline arrives
outlets.summary: WaPo covers Trump's declaration that 'hostilities have terminated' for War Powers Resolution purposes. Sen. Tim Kaine: 'A ceasefire means bombs aren't dropping. It doesn't mean there are no hostilities.' Sen. Susan Collins to introduce AUMF May 11.

outlets.name: CNN
outlets.headline: Two months into the Iran war, almost everybody is a loser
outlets.summary: CNN analysis argues most parties have lost after 2 months — US bases damaged, Iran infrastructure devastated, oil at $108, 61% of Americans call war a mistake. China may emerge as geopolitical winner.

outlets.name: NPR
outlets.headline: Republicans defer to Trump on Iran war despite War Powers deadline

outlets.name: Washington Examiner
outlets.headline: Is it a war? A stalemate? A quagmire? What to call the Iran conflict
outlets.summary: Washington Examiner argues it's 'a war on hiatus,' not a quagmire. Notes Trump instructed aides to prepare for extended blockade. Right-leaning frame: Trump maintaining leverage through maximum pressure.

outlets.name: Washington Times
outlets.headline: U.S. optimistic the blockade will force Iran to reopen Hormuz
outlets.summary: Washington Times leads with administration's economic warfare success — Iran exports collapsed to 567K bpd, costing Iran ~$500M/day. Frames blockade as winning strategy.

outlets.name: Al Jazeera
outlets.headline: Iran war: What's happening on Day 64 as Trump rejects Tehran's proposal
outlets.summary: Al Jazeera comprehensive Day 64 roundup: Trump rejects Iran proposal, ~50 Israeli airstrikes kill 7+ in S. Lebanon, Ford carrier departs, CENTCOM requests Dark Eagle hypersonic, 61% say war was mistake, Iran warns conflict 'likely' to restart.

outlets.name: Al Jazeera
outlets.headline: Has the US-Iran ceasefire reset the clock on War Powers Act deadline?
outlets.summary: Al Jazeera analytical piece on Trump's 'hostilities have terminated' legal claim. Cites legal scholars saying the ceasefire does not pause the WPR clock; blockade itself constitutes hostilities.

outlets.name: Times of Israel
outlets.headline: IDF soldier killed, 12 wounded in Hezbollah drone attacks; ~50 strikes hit south Lebanon
outlets.summary: ToI leads with IDF Sgt. Liem Ben Hamo (19, Golani Brigade) killed by Hezbollah drone. 12 soldiers wounded at Shomera artillery position. IDF launches ~50 airstrikes across S. Lebanon. Hezbollah fiber-optic FPV drone campaign: 27+ attacks documented.

outlets.name: Jerusalem Post
outlets.headline: IDF shifts focus to Iranian nuclear threat as Trump briefed on CENTCOM plan to renew Iran strikes
outlets.summary: JPost frames CENTCOM briefing and IDF preparations as renewed military pressure. Explicitly anti-Iran, supportive of strike resumption.

outlets.name: PressTV
outlets.headline: Iran's resilience pays off with major strategic gains as US pressure tactics fail
outlets.summary: State-run PressTV frames Iran as achieving strategic victories. Khamenei's 'disgraceful defeat' rhetoric amplified. Araghchi 'open to diplomacy if US drops excessive demands.' Iran casting itself as winning the narrative war.

outlets.name: Mehr News
outlets.headline: Araghchi briefs regional FMs on Iran's diplomatic position
outlets.summary: Mehr reports Araghchi's phone calls with FMs of Turkey, Egypt, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, Iraq, and Azerbaijan. Tehran frames itself as the diplomacy-ready party while US escalates blockade.
